gold_final_answer: P(x) = a x(x-1)(x+1) for any real a
prompt:
Find all polynomials $P(x)$ such that the equality
$$
(x - 1)P(x + 1) - (x + 1)P(x - 1) = 4P(x)
$$
holds for all real $x$.
response_text:
We look for all polynomials \(P(x)\) satisfying
\[
(x-1)P(x+1)-(x+1)P(x-1)=4P(x)\qquad \forall x\in\mathbb R.
\]

Let \(\deg P=n\) and write
\[
P(x)=a_n x^n+\cdots,\qquad a_n\neq 0.
\]

We compare leading terms. Using
\[
P(x\pm1)=a_n(x\pm1)^n+\cdots = a_n\left(x^n \pm nx^{n-1}+\cdots\right),
\]
we get
\[
(x-1)P(x+1)=(x-1)a_n\left(x^n+nx^{n-1}+\cdots\right)
= a_n\left(x^{n+1}+(n-1)x^n+\cdots\right),
\]
and
\[
(x+1)P(x-1)=(x+1)a_n\left(x^n-nx^{n-1}+\cdots\right)
= a_n\left(x^{n+1}-(n-1)x^n+\cdots\right).
\]
Subtracting,
\[
(x-1)P(x+1)-(x+1)P(x-1)=2a_n(n-1)x^n+\cdots.
\]
The right-hand side is
\[
4P(x)=4a_n x^n+\cdots.
\]
Hence
\[
2(n-1)=4 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad n=3.
\]
So any nonzero solution must be a cubic polynomial.

Now write
\[
P(x)=ax^3+bx^2+cx+d.
\]
Substitute into the identity. A direct expansion gives:
\[
(x-1)P(x+1)-(x+1)P(x-1)=4ax^3+4bx^2+4cx-4a.
\]
The right-hand side is
\[
4P(x)=4ax^3+4bx^2+4cx+4d.
\]
Comparing constants,
\[
-4a=4d \quad\Longrightarrow\quad d=-a.
\]
No other restrictions appear.

Therefore all solutions are
\[
\boxed{P(x)=ax^3+bx^2+cx-a \quad \text{for arbitrary real } a,b,c.}
\]

Final answer: \(\boxed{P(x)=ax^3+bx^2+cx-a\text{ for arbitrary real }a,b,c}\)
